1.description: External positions of German banks This figure shows the evolution of total external positions owned by German banks with counterparties in countries that have never been sanctioned (left-hand graph) or have been sanctioned at least once between 2002 and 2015 (right-hand graph). Positions are aggregated over all counterparties, asset classes, and currencies of denomination. They are broken down into total external assets of all banks located inside Germany (DEU), of all German bank affiliates abroad, and of the subset of German bank affiliates that are located outside the EU or outside the FATF. Sources: Research Data and Service Centre (RDSC) of the Deutsche Bundesbank, the external position of banks (AUSTA), from March 2002 to December 2015, and the authors’ own calculations.

2.description: Banks’ external positions around the introduction of financial sanctions This figure shows the evolution of banks’ external positions around the imposition of financial sanctions. The horizontal axes show event time measured in months. The vertical axis in panel A shows cumulative log asset growth Log assetsb,ct−Log assetsb,c−12 of the average bank since the year before the imposition of financial sanctions. The vertical axis in panel B shows estimated external positions conditionally on bank-time (⁠ααb,t⁠) and bank-counterparty country fixed effects (⁠ααb,c⁠). The positions are estimated based on the regression in Equation (1) and calculated relative to the time when financial sanctions are imposed (event time =0⁠). The solid lines in panels A and B represent banks that are located inside Germany whereas the dashed lines represent German bank affiliates located abroad. Sources: Research Data and Service Centre (RDSC) of the Deutsche Bundesbank, the external position of banks (AUSTA), from March 2002 to December 2015, and the authors’ own calculations.
